issues: rim_teal_accent: alternating teal/blue pixels on the coin rim feel slightly off-palette for a warm gold treasure — teal reads as cool/metallic rather than warm Hyrule gold
small_size_noise: the alternating orange-teal rim pattern collapses into visual noise at native 1x scale, reducing read clarity
star_center_low_contrast: white star on light-orange coin body loses definition at small size
reasoning: Emotional axis (60%): Strong warm orange-gold hue with a star motif — immediately readable as friendly Zelda-style treasure. Round silhouette, saturated warm palette, and the star emblem all align with the bright/charming Hyrule mood. The teal accent is the only emotional stumble — it introduces a cool note that slightly undercuts the all-warm toy-diorama feel. Emotional score ~7.5. Technical axis (40%): 1px hard outlines are present and consistent. Shading is 2–3 hard tones on the coin body (dark orange shadow at bottom, mid orange body, light/white highlight star). Pixels are grid-snapped with no visible anti-aliasing on the main form. The alternating teal-orange rim pattern is pixel-clean but the teal color choice is questionable — it reads more like a design quirk than deliberate hard shading in the Denzi tradition. Denzi reference items show extremely deliberate, minimal pixel placement with no color noise; the coin's rim pattern is noisier by comparison. Technical score ~6. Combined: 0.6×7.5 + 0.4×6 = 4.5 + 2.4 = 6.9 → 7.
